home.explore_p1: Discover the beauty and attractions of Naviraí, a city rich in culture, nature, and hospitality. From lush parks to vibrant events, there is something for everyone to enjoy.
home.explore_p2: Naviraí is located in the southeast region of the state, being considered an important regional agro-industrial and service hub, known as the
home.explore_p3: Founded in 1952, the city has a modern infrastructure and is the ideal starting point for exploring the region's tourist potential.

invest_widget.title: Invest in Naviraí
invest_widget.description: Discover why Naviraí is the ideal location for your next major investment. A hub of growth and opportunity.

festa_junina.name: Festa Junina
festa_junina.description: Traditional celebration with typical food, square dancing, and lots of fun.

music_festival.name: Music Festival
music_festival.description: Performances by local and national bands in an unmissable event for music lovers.

craft_fair.name: Craft Fair

hero.title: Our History
hero.subtitle: From a Planned Town to the Capital of the Conesul

introduction.title: Naviraí
introduction.subtitle: The March to the West
introduction.p1: The history of Naviraí is an exemplary study of the interiorization policies known as the "March to the West". Unlike many spontaneous settlements, Naviraí was not born by chance.
introduction.p2: The municipality was conceived in a planned manner in 1952 by the Colonizadora Vera Cruz, with a peculiar urban layout in the shape of a "spider web", reflecting a high degree of organization and anticipation of growth.
introduction.p3: Strategically located in the Paraná River Basin, the city was born isolated, depending on river transport on the Amambai River, until the first roads were opened, allowing for the transport of timber and the arrival of settlers.

pioneers.caption: The beginning of logging in the 1950s.
pioneers.title: The Pioneer Years (1952-1958)
pioneers.p1: The initial nucleus, named Povoado Vera Cruz, was driven by the sawmills installed in 1953. Figures like Moryoshi Fukuda and Antônio Augusto dos Santos were essential in this phase.
pioneers.p2: An important cultural milestone occurred in 1961, with the founding of the Japanese Colony, which brought 25 families who introduced vital agricultural techniques for the future of the region.

item1.title: Founding
item1.desc: Start of colonization by Vera Cruz Ltda.

item2.title: District
item2.desc: Elevated to a District by Law No. 1,195.

item3.title: Municipality
item3.desc: Emancipation via Law No. 1,944, by Weimar Torres.

item4.title: Installation
item4.desc: Inauguration of the 1st Mayor, João Martins Cardoso.

card1.title: The Timber Cycle
card1.desc: Dominated by extraction and sawmills. It was the period of initial settlement and the opening of the first transport routes.

card2.title: Diversification
card2.desc: The 70s and 80s. Expansion of livestock farming and the peak of the cotton cycle, modernizing the agricultural base.

card3.title: Agro-industry
card3.desc: Post-1980 with Proálcool, sugarcane mills, and more recently, high technology in soy and corn.

today.title: Naviraí Today
today.desc: Currently, Naviraí transcends its role as an agro-industrial hub. With robust infrastructure, such as the BR-163 highway, and investments in education and services, the city has consolidated itself as the regional support center, ensuring the sustainable development of the Cone-Sul of Mato Grosso do Sul.

hero.title: Invest in Naviraí
hero.subtitle: A hub of opportunities in the heart of Brazil.

introduction.title: Why Naviraí?
introduction.subtitle: Growth, Innovation, and Quality of Life.
introduction.p1: Naviraí represents a center of opportunities for investors seeking a dynamic business environment, with expanding infrastructure and a strategic geographical location.
introduction.p2: The city offers a unique combination of market access, natural resources, and a hardworking community, making it an ideal destination for new ventures and industrial expansion.

sectors.alt: Agricultural machinery in a soybean field
sectors.title: Strategic Sectors
sectors.p1: Agribusiness is the backbone of the local economy, with emphasis on the production of soy, corn, and sugarcane. Industrialization, focused on the transformation of agricultural products, and a constantly growing trade and services sector complement the economic scenario.
sectors.p2: Tax incentives and a favorable business environment are available for companies wishing to establish themselves in the city's main industrial hubs.

card1.title: Privileged Logistics
card1.desc: Quick access to important highways (BR-163) and proximity to Mercosur markets.

card2.title: Growing Ecosystem
card2.desc: Strong synergy between cooperatives, producers, and industries, fostering innovation and development.

card3.title: Support and Incentives
card3.desc: Public policies to attract investment and support local entrepreneurs.

cta.title: Be Part of Naviraí's Future
cta.desc: Contact the city hall to learn about incentive projects and discover how your business can thrive in one of the fastest-growing cities in the state.
